senses.definition: Automatic or self‑regulated standardization of processes or products, often using technology to keep features the same without manual control.
examples: The factory introduced autostandardization to keep product quality consistent across all lines.
In modern manufacturing, autostandardization reduces human error and speeds up production.

senses.definition: A device or system that causes equipment, especially a machine or program, to start automatically without user action.
examples: The software includes an autostarter that launches the app when the computer boots.
An autostarter can ensure critical monitoring tools are always running in the background.

senses.definition: A modern stethoscope that uses automatic or digital features, such as sound amplification or signal processing, to help listen to body sounds.
examples: The doctor used an autostethoscope to record and analyze the patient's heart sounds.
An autostethoscope can filter background noise to make lung sounds easier to hear.

senses.definition: A person who travels by asking passing drivers for free rides; a hitchhiker.
examples: We picked up an autostoper on the road outside the village.
The autostoper carried a small backpack and a cardboard sign with his destination.

senses.definition: A wide, divided highway or motorway in Italy, built for fast traffic between major cities.
examples: They drove north on the autostrada from Rome to Florence.
Tolls on the autostrada can be quite expensive during the holiday season.

senses.definition: Plural of autostrada: wide, divided highways or motorways in Italy used for fast traffic.
examples: The main Italian cities are linked by a network of modern autostradas.
Driving on the autostradas is usually faster than taking local roads.

senses.definition: Plural of autostrada in Italian; used in English to refer to Italian divided highways or motorways.
examples: The autostrade are known for their high speed limits and long straight sections.
Tourists often use the autostrade to move quickly between Italian regions.

senses.definition: Relating to a type of jaw attachment in some vertebrates where the upper jaw is firmly fused to the skull.
examples: Autostylic jaw suspension is characteristic of many tetrapods.
In autostylic species, the upper jaw is directly attached to the braincase.

senses.definition: The practice or tendency of creating a personal style for oneself, especially in art, writing, or fashion.
examples: Her paintings show a clear autostylism that ignores current trends.
Autostylism can help an artist develop a recognizable visual identity.

senses.definition: A type of jaw structure in some vertebrates where the upper jaw is fused to the skull rather than being loosely attached.
examples: Autostyly is one of several patterns of jaw suspension described in vertebrate evolution.
The emergence of autostyly marks a key change in the skull anatomy of early tetrapods.

senses.definition: To deliberately repeat thoughts or ideas to yourself in order to influence your feelings, behavior, or physical state.
examples: She tried to autosuggest calmness before the exam by repeating positive phrases.
Athletes often autosuggest confidence to improve their performance.

senses.definition: Past form of autosuggest: deliberately influenced your own thoughts or feelings by repeating certain ideas to yourself.
examples: He autosuggested a sense of control to cope with his anxiety.
They had autosuggested healthier habits over several months.

senses.definition: The degree to which a person is able or likely to influence their own thoughts and feelings by repeated self‑suggestion.
examples: High autosuggestibility can make self-help techniques more effective.
The study examined the relationship between autosuggestibility and pain tolerance.

senses.definition: Able or likely to affect your own thoughts and feelings strongly through repeated self‑suggestion.
examples: Highly autosuggestible individuals may respond better to self-hypnosis exercises.
Children can be particularly autosuggestible when exposed to repeated messages.

senses.definition: Continuously repeating ideas to yourself in order to change your thoughts, feelings, or behavior.
examples: She walked into the meeting autosuggesting the phrase, "I am prepared and confident."
Autosuggesting positive outcomes can sometimes reduce stress.

senses.definition: A mental technique in which you repeat ideas to yourself to change your thoughts, feelings, or physical reactions.
examples: Through autosuggestion, he gradually overcame his fear of flying.
Autosuggestion is often used in self-hypnosis and relaxation training.

senses.definition: A person who studies, teaches, or actively uses autosuggestion as a method for personal change or therapy.
examples: An autosuggestionist may guide clients to repeat helpful phrases during sessions.
The famous autosuggestionist wrote several books on the power of the subconscious mind.

senses.definition: Plural of autosuggestion: repeated thoughts or phrases said to yourself to influence how you think or feel.
examples: Her daily autosuggestions focused on gratitude and confidence.
The program teaches users to design effective autosuggestions for their goals.

senses.definition: Relating to or using autosuggestion to influence thoughts or feelings.
examples: The course includes autosuggestive exercises to build self-esteem.
Autosuggestive techniques can support other forms of therapy.

senses.definition: Third person singular of autosuggest: repeatedly tells themselves particular ideas to influence their mind or behavior.
examples: He autosuggests success before every important presentation.
She regularly autosuggests a sense of peace to manage stress.

senses.definition: Self‑directed control or reduction of a process, activity, or reaction, often through internal regulation or feedback.
examples: The immune system's autosuppression can prevent excessive inflammatory responses.
Autosuppression of negative thoughts is a key aim of some cognitive therapies.

senses.definition: Relating to a symbiotic relationship that is self‑maintained or occurs within a single organism.
examples: The organism exhibits autosymbiontic behavior under stressful conditions.
Autosymbiontic processes may reduce dependence on external partners.

senses.definition: Relating to symbols that arise from a person's own mind and represent aspects of their inner life.
examples: The dream contained autosymbolic images linked to her childhood.
Autosymbolic fantasies often reflect unconscious wishes.

senses.definition: Having the quality of expressing inner thoughts or feelings through symbols generated by the self.
examples: Her poem is deeply autosymbolical, full of private references.
Autosymbolical narratives can be difficult for others to interpret.

senses.definition: In a way that uses self‑generated symbols to express inner thoughts or feelings.
examples: The character speaks autosymbolically, turning personal pain into myth.
Dreams often function autosymbolically rather than literally.

senses.definition: A rare term for self‑communication or inner dialogue within a person’s own mind.
examples: The philosopher described autosymnoia as a constant background of thought.
In meditation, autosymnoia may become calmer and more focused.

senses.definition: A brand or technical name used for certain automatic synchronizing devices or systems, especially in engineering.
examples: The control panel uses an autosyn to transmit position data to the indicator.
Maintenance of the autosyn is critical for accurate instrument readings.

senses.definition: Self‑joining or self‑linking, especially referring to the pairing of chromosomes with themselves rather than with a different set.
examples: Autosyndesis can occur in certain types of polyploid plants.
The geneticist observed autosyndesis during meiosis under the microscope.

senses.definition: A rare technical term whose precise meaning depends on a specialized field, often referring to something that acts on itself.
examples: The researcher used the term autota to describe a self-directed regulatory factor.
In this model, an autota influences its own production over time.

senses.definition: An automatic telegraph device that can send messages without continuous manual control.
examples: The autotelegraph allowed messages to be transmitted more quickly than traditional systems.
Engineers improved the autotelegraph to reduce transmission errors.

senses.definition: Done for its own sake, because the activity itself is rewarding, not because of an outside goal.
examples: For many artists, painting is an autotelic activity.
He described learning as autotelic when it is driven by curiosity rather than grades.

senses.definition: The belief or quality of an activity being valuable in itself, not as a means to another goal.
examples: Autotelism is central to some theories of art, which see art as worthwhile for its own sake.
The book defends autotelism in education, arguing that learning should be enjoyable in itself.

senses.definition: A machine at a bank where customers can withdraw or deposit money using a card and PIN; an ATM.
examples: She stopped at the autoteller to get some cash before dinner.
Many autotellers now allow you to deposit checks without an envelope.

senses.definition: Plural of autoteller: bank machines that customers use to handle basic money transactions.
examples: The bank installed more autotellers to reduce lines inside the branch.
Autotellers are available in supermarkets and gas stations across the city.

senses.definition: A test that you can perform on yourself or on your own equipment without help from a professional.
examples: The kit includes an autotest you can use at home to check for the virus.
The software runs an autotest at startup to detect common problems.

senses.definition: Plural of autotest: tests that can be carried out by a person on themselves or automatically by a system.
examples: Regular autotests can help patients monitor their condition between doctor visits.
The device performs several autotests before starting normal operation.

senses.definition: An organism, especially a plant, that has four copies of each chromosome set, all coming from the same species.
examples: The researcher compared the autotetraploid to its diploid ancestor.
Autotetraploid plants may show larger cell size and different traits.

senses.definition: Plural of autotetraploidy: cases or conditions in which organisms have four copies of their chromosome set from the same species.
examples: The paper discussed several autotetraploidies found in wild plant populations.
Autotetraploidies can arise spontaneously or be induced by chemicals.

senses.definition: Plural of autotetraploid: organisms with four chromosome sets derived from a single species.
examples: The field contained both diploids and autotetraploids of the same grass species.
Autotetraploids often show differences in fertility compared with diploids.

senses.definition: The condition of having four copies of each chromosome set that all originate from the same species.
examples: Autotetraploidy is an important mechanism in the evolution of some crop plants.
The genetic consequences of autotetraploidy differ from those of allopolyploidy.

senses.definition: A person who claims to perform wonders or miracles by their own power.
examples: He was regarded as an autothaumaturgist by his followers.
The novel features an autothaumaturgist who heals people with a touch.

senses.definition: A drive‑in cinema where people watch films from their own cars.
examples: The old autotheater reopened with classic movies from the 1950s.
At the autotheater, visitors listen to the film through their car radios.

senses.definition: The belief that one is divine or that the self is a god.
examples: Autotheism appears in certain mystical traditions that identify the self with the divine.
Critics saw his philosophy as a form of autotheism.

senses.definition: Plural of autotheism: different beliefs or doctrines that treat the self as divine.
examples: The scholar compared various autotheisms across cultures.
Modern spiritual movements sometimes develop new autotheisms.

senses.definition: A person who believes that they themselves are divine or godlike.
examples: He was criticized as an autotheist for claiming to possess absolute truth.
The character in the novel becomes an autotheist after a spiritual crisis.

senses.definition: Plural of autotheist: people who believe themselves to be divine.
examples: The sect's leaders were described as autotheists by outside observers.
Throughout history, several autotheists have attracted devoted followings.

senses.definition: Providing therapy or healing through the person’s own actions or processes rather than outside treatment.
examples: Meditation can be seen as an autotherapeutic practice.
The author describes writing a diary as autotherapeutic.

senses.definition: Treatment that a person gives to themselves, often using mental or behavioral techniques to improve their health.
examples: Cognitive autotherapy can help people change unhelpful thought patterns.
The manual offers guidance on using autotherapy between counseling sessions.

senses.definition: The ability of an organism to produce and control its own body heat.
examples: Autothermy allows mammals to remain active in cold environments.
The evolution of autothermy was a major step in vertebrate history.

senses.definition: A timing device on a camera or other equipment that starts or stops an action automatically after a set delay.
examples: We used the autotimer on the camera to take a group photo.
Set the autotimer so the lights turn off after ten minutes.
